summary: Product acceptance is traceable from OCR-S-1..8 through the Architecture and canonical Spec to production owners and current-HEAD E2E. The guarded DAG excludes autonomous PR creation, merge, waiver, and material external effects; each remains an explicit human operation. This role verdict is not PR or merge authorization.
inspection_summary: Confirmed the Story-to-Architecture-to-Spec clauses, the canonical guarded action registry and boundary rejection, the focused product/E2E assertions, and strict-head verification evidence for 66131d96a869fc317519a66c13ea0e3b0b8d3e1d.
inspection_evidence: src/one-command-pr-ready-closure.js: createOneCommandPrReadyActionOwners allowlists callback boundaries and final_prepare requires current-HEAD ready_for_pr_create
src/safe-action-orchestrator.js: AUTONOMOUS_REGISTRY contains only diagnose, prepare_artifacts, implement, verify, review, repair, final_prepare
test/one-command-pr-ready-closure.test.js: OCR-T-2 rejects createPullRequest, mergePullRequest, grantWaiver, deploy, publish, and performMaterialExternalSideEffect without invocation
test/e2e/story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure-runtime.spec.ts: S-001 through S-005 cover production-shaped repair/re-review and explicit authority boundary
judgment_delta: Initial concern: a one-command implementation loop could implicitly cross PR, merge, waiver, or external-side-effect authority and leave acceptance coverage on a happy path only.

Final judgment: pass because the Story, Architecture, and canonical Spec make those operations human-only; runtime boundary allowlisting plus focused negative assertions reject every supplied authority seam before invocation; the strict-head E2E evidence covers repair/re-review, typed stops, current-HEAD final prepare, and absent autonomous pr_create/merge/waiver actions.
